Off Calendar Mar-29-2011 Continued To Jun-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Bassin for and with Wallace Szmokaluk. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administator (SFPA). Louise Mendoza for the listing office.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the report by Mr. Bassin, that the matter has lingered because of the large IRS loan (etc), that some of the tenants will vacate by 4/1/11, that the petition states that the sale is subject to approval of the IRS, that the matter is not progressing, and that it wanted to know if Mr. Szmokaluk should be removed. Mr. Bassin stated that he included the language regarding the approval by the IRS of the sale because he could not see how they could close escrow if the IRS doesn't approve the amount. Ms. Mendoza stated that since the price has been reduced that they have received a lot of requests for disclosure packets which is good in this market, that the prior listing amount was not in line with the market and they could not get interested buyers, and that they expect to receive offers now. Mr. Bassin stated that they have made numerous efforts to settle, that the IRS won't negotiate at all, and that Mr. Szmokaluk could continue on in his role as Executor. Mr. Ferdon stated that the SFPA would not want to get involved in a lawsuit if sought after. The Court stated that it would continue the matter again for 60 days to see what offers come in now that the tenant is out and that due to the lack of progress in this matter that it may consider removing Mr. Szmokaluk. Status on the sale of the property and on the removal of the Executor is set for June 7, 2011. Mr. Bassin confirmed that if they get an offer that he will petition and inquired about republishing without the IRS language. The Court stated that it would leave the issue of republishing up to Mr. Bassin.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ATTY. JONATHAN FERDON, COURT APPOINTED ATTORNEY;

THE COURT'S CONCERN IS ABOUT NO PROPER NOTICE WERE GIVEN TO THE PARTIES AND OF THE PETITIONER'S NOT HIRING AN ATTORNEY. MR. BASSIN WAS REQUESTING THAT THIS MATTER BE SENT TO MEDIATION SINCE THERE IS A FAMILY LAW ISSUE STILL UNRESOLVED AS TO ENTITLEMENT TO HER CLAIM. MR. Off Calendar Feb-15-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Bassin (Counsel for Wallace Szmokaluk, Personal Representative); Natalia Nemechayeff (Creditor in pro per).

The Court noted at the outset that it was in receipt of the Stipulation which resolved the substantive issues of the trial scheduled for this date. Mr. Bassin confirmed this understanding and as a result the trial and Petition to Determine Distributive Rights was taken off calendar.

As to the status of the final distribution, also on calendar, Mr. Bassin reported that occupancy issues with the real property, the sole asset of the estate, had been resolved and the property was now in a position to be sold. However, since it is the sole asset of the estate, no distribution could be made in the absence of its sale for which efforts have been initiated. Ms. Nemechayeff expressed certain concerns to the sale price and efforts made to sell the property thus far. Further discussion was had as to preliminary distribution options once the property was sold.

Inasmuch as the original probate appraisal of the property was over one year old, the Court ordered the Executor to secure a new appraisal at the earliest possible time, preferably before the next hearing on February 26, 2007.
